title: Saying how you feel
setting: You and Lucía have been together a while. It's a quiet, tender moment, and you want to tell her what she means to you.

steps.speaker: Lucía
steps.en: You're very quiet. What are you thinking about?

options.es: En lo feliz que soy con vos.
options.en: About how happy I am with you.
options.feedback: Open and warm — sharing the happy thought invites closeness. Perfect.
A missed chance to connect. She's inviting you in — step through the door.
Simple and intimate. 'En nosotros' says a lot in two words.

steps.speaker: Lucía
steps.en: Oh yeah? And what do you think about us?

options.es: Que te quiero más cada día.
options.en: That I care for you more each day.
options.feedback: 'Te quiero' for tender affection — exactly the right register here. Lovely.
Way too lukewarm for the moment! She's opening her heart — meet her there.

options.es: Que sos muy importante para mí.
options.en: That you're very important to me.
options.feedback: Sincere and heartfelt. A beautiful thing to say and easy to mean.

steps.speaker: Lucía
steps.es: Vos también sos todo para mí.

options.es: Quiero que sepás que te amo.
options.en: I want you to know that I love you.
options.feedback: The subjunctive 'que sepás' plus 'te amo' — the deepest declaration. Brave and beautiful.
'Same' to 'you're everything to me'?! This moment deserves your real words.

options.es: Me hacés muy feliz, de verdad.
options.en: You make me very happy, truly.
options.feedback: Heartfelt and genuine. 'De verdad' adds sincerity. Wonderful.

steps.speaker: Lucía
steps.es: Yo también te amo. Con toda el alma.
steps.en: I love you too. With all my soul.

options.es: Ojalá siempre estemos así de bien.
options.en: I hope we're always this good together.
options.feedback: 'Ojalá... estemos' — subjunctive of hope, the perfect tender wish. Masterful.
'Mi amor' is the natural term of endearment — sweet and fitting.
Don't shut down the most tender moment! Let it breathe.
